TextENGNew: "W-wait a second, Minai-san!"
Ignoring Fujita's repeated pleas to stop, Tomoe stepped out of the car and marched straight toward the crime scene. Alarmed by her behavior, he rushed ahead and planted himself in her path.
"What the hell are you doing, Minai-san?!"
"Get out of my way..."
Her quiet glare — seething with barely contained fury — made Fujita's breath catch in his throat.
changeReason: Old mistranslates 一瞥 (a glance/look) as 'remark' -- wrong sensory channel.

TextENGNew: What on earth had come over her...?
...In all the time Fujita had known her, he had never once seen such fury on Tomoe's face.
It had been just over two months since Section Chief Yamamura had assigned him to work under Tomoe Minai — a female detective two ranks above him, despite being the same age.

She was sharp, but had a surprisingly childlike side. Her natural charm made her far more approachable than the typical elite — less the untouchable prodigy, more the diligent, attentive class representative.
So even when she shouted and lashed out, he would step in to restrain her — half in jest, half in earnest — and somehow they made it work.
changeReason: Old misinterprets 半分じゃれあいも入って as 'stop her halfway'; new correctly conveys 'half in jest.'

TextENGNew: But not now. Right now, he couldn't do that. He couldn't even get close to her.
If her usual anger was like being struck with a wooden sword... this was a bared blade.
A killing intent hung in the air — the sense that if he took one more step closer, he would genuinely not survive.
changeReason: New translation renders 凶気 (kyouki) more precisely as 'killing intent' rather than the vaguer 'viciousness,' and adds the visceral sense of mortal danger present in the Japanese.

TextENGNew: "M-Minai-san...?"
Tomoe slipped past the frozen Fujita without a word.
changeReason: Old mistranslates すり抜ける (to slip past) as 'walked by her side' -- different action.

TextENGNew: Two detectives from the Second Division who had been managing the crowd of onlookers noticed their approach and hurried over.
"Wh-why are you here, Assistant Director Minai?!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_keijiA2026. This scene is under the Second Investigation Division's control."
"If you step in now, you'll be compromising our authority!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_keijiB2021. If you get in the way of our investigation..."
"...Out of the way...!!"

Overwhelmed by her aura, the two Second Division detectives who had tried to block her path stepped back and cleared the way. Tomoe passed them without so much as a glance, striding toward the crime scene.
"M-Minai-san...!"
Still bewildered, Fujita followed in Tomoe's wake and stepped into the crime scene.
...Forensic investigation was being conducted and evidence photos were being taken throughout the site, while red police lights flashed all around them.
And, behind the crowd of people tirelessly working... was a slightly bulging sheet laying on the ground.
When Tomoe began walking in that direction, a hand grabbed her from the side.
"Princess?@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hayami2002. What's going on? You're not supposed to be here."
"...Please excuse me, Hayami-san.@k@vS11/28/420101151. I'll be out of here soon."
"H-hey..."
With that, Tomoe bowed her head to her acquaintance from the mobile investigation team, and then she lifted up the sheet without waiting for an answer.
Until now, she'd thought she was getting used to seeing corpses.
It takes a change of heart to be able to look on at that miserable state of human life.
But.
But right now...!!
"Princess...?"
She clenched her fists so intensely... that her nails bit into her palms.
A dull sound rang out as she clenched her teeth.
Then, sensing the warm, rusty smell emanating from her mouth, Tomoe wiped at her lip.
It was a slimy liquid. ...She was biting down on her lip without thinking, and she bit hard enough to cut into the meat and spout blood.
"...Thank you so much, Minai-san."
She had a bright smile on her face when they parted ways... The image still lingered on the back of her eyelids.
She was such a cute girl.
She was a strong girl, who wanted to put the past to rest and finally move forward.
And yet.
Never again... would she be able to see her face, to have a conversation together.
"Minai-san... did you know her?"
"...@k@vS11/28/420101155.I met her just a little while ago... and we talked.@k@vS11/28/420101155_1. She was... such a cute girl...!"
Trying her hardest to suppress her raging emotions... Tomoe said that, and moaned.
Really... really cute! ...She surely would've become a beautiful woman in the future!!
She lamented that it wouldn't come to be... And regretted it...!!
"It's best not to look too much, Princess.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hayami2007. ...I rushed here as soon as I got the report, but it was already like this."

The mobile investigation team detective, Hayami, put a hand on Tomoe's shoulder as he said that. After taking a deep breath to cool her head, Tomoe folded the sheet down, then held her hands together and closed her eyes.
"...What about the driver?"

"He was found at the site, in utter shock.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hayami2009. The culprit was taken to the prefectural police department's workplace negligence unit.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hayami2010. At the moment, the investigation is being handled by the First Investigation Division at HQ, rather than the transportation division."
"...First Investigation Division?"
Hayami nodded.
The truck was allegedly driving at normal speed, but the driver only at the last second noticed the shadow of a person on the ground in a shaded area where the streetlights couldn't reach.
Then, the driver quickly turned the wheel and slammed on the brakes, but couldn't get away...

"The brake markings corroborate that testimony.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hayami2012. On-site investigation is still ongoing, but it's hard to believe that someone would notice a person on the ground and deliberately strike them."
"Moreover, this location was chosen because it was far from the streetlights and hard to see, whether she deliberately chose to jump into traffic... or was "set" down there."
"Considering that nobody would ever want to lay down in the road, that probability seems high."
"...So, is the First Division even going to bother with it?"

"The victim is a schoolgirl, so the media is going to eat it up.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hayami2016. Considering the potential news coverage, Section Chief Doujima decided it would be best for HQ to handle it.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hayami2017. ...The body is also supposed to be submitted for autopsy immediately."
"Is that so...?"
"More importantly, Princess, you should get out of here right away.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hayami2019. This area is under the Second Division's authority..."
Unfortunately, as Hayami whispered that to Tomoe, Section Chief Okabe and Kojima from the Second Division had arrived.
"...What are you doing here?"
"You realize that the Second Division is in charge of this location... right, Assistant Director Minai?"
After a quick glance at the two of them and their magnanimous demeanor, She turned away from the sheet and rose to her feet.
Okabe stepped between her and Hayami as if to say they were in the way, and made Kojima lift the sheet.
"...I can hardly bear to look.@k@vS11/00/421100025. There's not even a trace of how she looked when she still lived, huh."
"I'll give you the photos later, so let's leave it at that."
Having said that, Hayami somewhat forcefully began laying the sheet back down. ...He was being considerate of Tomoe.
"So, what can you tell us about the incident?"

"An official report will be provided after formal autopsy.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hayami2022. The forensics team believes the victim was alive up until she was struck, and we are planning to conduct this investigation led by the First Division at HQ."

"Hmph, the media will be happy to jump on an incident like this.@k@vS11/00/421100028. If it's a suicide, they'll say she was overworked studying for exams, or fell to despair from a broken heart. If it's murder, they'll peg it as a grudge or assault.@k@vS11/00/421100029. ...I'd love to be the person who has to clean this up."
Tomoe did everything to resist the urge to shout at him and tell him to keep his mouth shut if he doesn't know the circumstances.

"Anyway, as long as the First Division takes over, there's no reason for us to take action.@k@vS11/00/421100031. ...After that, we can leave it to the community affairs division and it'll be out of our hands?"
"Please do. Thank you for your assistance."
"With that in mind, Assistant Director Minai, you all should be on your way.@k@vS11/00/421100033. ...If you came here expecting to steal our thunder, you were mistaken."
Tomoe wasn't interested in playing along with Okabe's sarcastic banter, so she pretended not to hear it and turned to Hayami.
"Hayami-san, please let me know the autopsy results.@k@vS11/28/420101163. And just in case, could I get a transcript of what the driver said?"
"Sure, got it."
"And if possible, anything left in this area..."

"Aah, if you would like anything else, you'll have to ask prefectural police through us.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_kojima2020. This incident is under the authority of the Second Division, so it could prove troublesome if you don't follow proper regulations."
Kojima stepped between Tomoe and Hayami as he said that.
It was obvious to everyone that he was just saying that to earn points with Okabe, but Tomoe concealed her emotions and responded, "Of course," while nodding.
"We'll submit a formal request tomorrow after getting consent from the chief.@k@vS11/28/420101166. Sorry for the trouble, and thank you for your support."
"...I see. The fox is borrowing the tiger's authority.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_kojima2022. You want the glory that badly?"
Tomoe silently asked herself, "Which of us is the fox here?" but responded with a wry smile as though she didn't care.
However, Kojima kept going, seemingly spurned by the threat of incurring her boss's anger.

"Naturally, all you had to do to buy the chief's favor is give him your little sister.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_kojima2024. Use everything at your disposal, even your family... Oh my, executive candidates have a completely different way of looking at things."
"What's that...?"
"Have your sister marry into power and reap the rewards yourself.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_kojima2026. That's not an easy thing to accomplish. You've got some profound foresight."
"After all, it would be extremely difficult for a woman to get into your position otherwise."
...Normally, she would've cursed him in her heart and paid that comment no heed.
As a member of society, she intended to have the minimum level of patience and common sense expected of an adult.
But.
But today... right at this very moment...!
"Huh?"

"I said, shut up!@k@vS11/28/420101171. Are you not ashamed of what you're saying, you shithead?!@k@vS11/28/420101172. If you're blind to your own weaknesses and looking to pick a fight, I'm ready to fight back at any moment. Well?!"
"D-don't do this! Minai-san?!"

After she closed the distance between them and spoke up with a totally different tone, Kojima couldn't help but fall silent. ...If Fujita hadn't grabbed her arm so quickly, she might've taken it a step further.
There were rumors going around behind the scenes ever since she came to Kakiuchi Station. But since the rumors were completely ungrounded, Tomoe paid them no heed.
However, if someone were to make flippant remarks right in front of her rather than acting like a considerate, sensible adult, what could you expect...?
"Watch your tongue, Assistant Director Minai.@k@vS11/00/421100035. You mustn't speak that way to your elders."
"Then what does that say about Deputy Section Chief Kojima's remarks?@k@vS11/31/421200057. He clearly insulted Minai-san, so set things right!"

"He's saying that point of view is valid.@k@vS11/00/421100037. ...Anyway, even you can't feign ignorance of that.@k@vS11/00/421100038. Sisters will be sisters, after all. There's no disputing you have the same blood and lineage."
That line breached the end of Tomoe's patience.
She completely... flew off the handle.
"One more time... Try saying that one more time, come on!!@k@vS11/28/420101175. Don't you even know what is and isn't appropriate to say, you senile old man?!"
"H-hold on, Minai-san!!@k@vS11/31/421200059. Stop this, I'm begging you!!"
"Let me go, Fujita!!@k@vS11/28/420101177. That bastard... that bastard!!"
She couldn't remember well what happened after that.
But in the end, they were thrown out of the crime scene by nearby investigators.
The one thing she didn't forget... was the pain of Hayami hitting her and shouting, "Calm down."
"...So, a one-week suspension?@k@vS11/29/420400186. Honestly... you're a damn idiot, you know that?"
"Oh, shut up!"
Tomoe vented her irritation at Madoka who was sitting in bed, while she continued peeling the apple that she had brought for Madoka.
Madoka couldn't help but sigh while gazing at the dexterity of her handiwork.
"But I'm shocked you got away with a temporary suspension.@k@vS11/29/420400188. A detective punching a superior officer right in view of the public... you could've been fired if that had gone south."
"It was a slap, not a punch!"
"That doesn't make a difference. It's assault either way.@k@vS11/29/420400190. You're a police officer, not a soldier. You understand that, right, Sis?"
When her sister so bluntly pointed that out, Tomoe was completely at a loss for words.
Indeed, even in situations where the blood rushes to your head, resorting to violence is the lowest act a member of society can stoop to.
But... secretly, Madoka was a little happy about it.
"So, did you apologize properly?@k@vS11/29/420400192. To Section Chief Okabe?"
"...Yeah.@k@vS11/28/420101182. I thought he was going to say something offensive, but he just said to be careful in the future.@k@vS11/28/420101183. I wonder if he's planning something?"
"Maybe he was just afraid?@k@vS11/29/420400194. If he said something careless and upset you again, it might just start another act of Violence Theater."
"E-excuse you!@k@vS11/28/420101185. I don't fly off the handle for no reason like that!"
"Okay, you're definitely not convincing me of that."
"...Hmph."
Realizing there was nothing she could say to win this argument, Tomoe sullenly cut off her protest.
Then, she sliced the peeled apple into pieces, cut out the core, neatly set them on a plate, and inserted toothpicks into each before lightly holding the plate out to Madoka.
"Huh? You're not going to feed them to me?"
"...You've got two arms, don't you?"
"Come on, I'm seriously ill~."
"No seriously ill patient has ever looked, sounded, and eaten that energetically!@k@vS11/28/420101189. If you don't want them, I'll eat them all myself, ok?"
"Says the one who's gonna eat half either way..."

With that, Madoka took the small plate, grabbed one by the toothpick, and shoved it into her mouth. The strong sweet and sour, and the moderately soft texture of the fruit mixed together to be very delicious.
"Mmm~, the juicy texture is really tasty~♪.@k@vS11/29/420400200. Did you get these from that guy at the market in front of the hospital?"
"The shop beneath the department store, right in front of the station.@k@vS11/28/420101191. They were really expensive, so you'd better savor them."
"Got it. I owe you one."
"...Are you all right now?"

"Yeah, sorry.@k@vS11/29/420400203. ...I'm fine around the fire from the gas stove these days, so I lowered my guard a little.@k@vS11/29/420400204. It's been a long time since I've gotten this way, so even I was surprised."
She picked up another apple and cut it into eight half-moon shaped pieces. This one was unpeeled and intended to share with Madoka.
"...@k@vS11/29/420400206.Sis, I've always been making trouble for you.@k@vS11/29/420400207. Even I think I need to get better soon."

"There's no need to say that.@k@vS11/28/420101195. The doctor said even if you recover quickly, you shouldn't go back to work just yet.@k@vS11/28/420101196. Besides, I've never even once thought you were a burden."
"Still... I'm hurting the people around me like this.@k@vS11/29/420400209. Sis, you know about the circumstances so you're ok,@k@vS11/29/420400210. ...but what about Kaoru-san?"
"What about him...? Have you not talked to him?"
"Ah, ahaha...@k@vS11/29/420400212. I've been planning to talk to him about it, but even today I still haven't gotten around to it."
"...Haah. Unbelievable."
This time, it was Tomoe's turn to heave a sigh. ...Her tendency to act immediately without thinking made things difficult at times like this.
And Tomoe couldn't act like it wasn't her issue either.

"I'm not saying this is all because of some boyfriend I was dating before, but...@k@vS11/29/420400214. In the end, things didn't go well.@k@vS11/29/420400215. People not used to it tend to be turned off by it."
"My part-time job at the restaurant didn't last long... thanks to a fire in the kitchen.@k@vS11/29/420400217. I'm ok around lighters though."
"...Madoka."
...She understood now. The reason she insisted on working at that cabaret club.
If she couldn't cheer herself up with alcohol, she wouldn't have been able to work...

"When I was admitted to this hospital, the official records said I took a blow to the head.@k@vS11/29/420400219. ...If I told him now, how would he react?@k@vS11/29/420400220. I know he's not the kind of person to say cruel things, but still..."
Seeing the deeply troubled look on Madoka's face... made Tomoe's chest ache.
Even after all this, her little sister was earnestly thinking about Yamaoki.
She still didn't know what Yamaoki's intentions were, but even so...
"...It's all right.@k@vS11/28/420101202. That man will be able to properly accept it."
"You think so...?"
"I do."
She put some more apples she finished coring on the small plate and bit into one while smiling to try to encourage her sister.

"If he wasn't the kind of person who cared about other people's problems, he never would've been able to get along with Dad as much as he did.@k@vS11/28/420101205. So please, talk to him properly in the future."
"You'll be fine as long as I'm in good health, but that's not always going to be the case, you know?"
"What is this?@k@vS11/29/420400222. Don't say something so ominous."
"...But in the one-in-10,000, no, one-in-100-million chance that does happen, I think you'd have an easier time enduring it if you were married to that man."
"You understand that much, right?"
"...Yeah."
Madoka nodded. ...She understood all that, but she just wanted to hear her sister say it.
Because it felt like she was just making excuses.
"...Aah, talking about this has made me feel better.@k@vS11/29/420400225. This is what compassionate and understanding elder sisters are for."
"Don't get full of yourself.@k@vS11/28/420101210. Plus, even if I accept your marriage, you won't get my approval unless you give it your all."
"Whoa, you're still saying..."
Just then.
They heard the clatter of the knob turning, and then a man dressed in a hospital gown emerged.
...His face didn't look familiar. And more importantly, there was no reason for another hospital patient to enter Madoka's private room.
So, what was the deal with this guy...?
"Who are you?"
"Huh? O-oh, umm..."
"Do you need something?!"
Tomoe quickly rose to her feet and stood in front of Madoka to cover her, reaching her hand into her jacket, then grimaced.
Since she was on suspension, she wasn't carrying her usual baton.
Still, at this distance, with one adversary... she could handle the situation if she closed the gap...!
"Oh, umm... Ah."
He suddenly donned a confused and upset expression when faced with that forceful question. Then, as if he had realized something, he took a step out of the room and took a look at the room tag.
"Ah... I-I'm sorry, I was mistaken!"
As soon as he tossed those words out, his face turned red and he slammed the door. Then, footsteps could be heard running down the hallway.
"...What was that?"
"Aah, he was probably just mistaken about which room this is.@k@vS11/29/420400229. Kaoru-san made the same mistake once before."
"Huh?"
Madoka lightly giggled... while staring at her sister's back. Tomoe was still standing in front of her trying to protect her. Then, Madoka poked her.
...According to Madoka, all the hospital rooms in this building from the third to eighth floor had the exact same layout.

And not only that, but there wasn't much variety in the furniture in the rooms or the view out the windows, so if you weren't used to it you could easily mistake which floor you're on after exiting the elevator.
Apparently Yamaoki came to visit earlier and mistakenly barged into another room on a lower floor without even knocking.
"...Now that you mention it, yeah."
Indeed, after bringing it up, Tomoe also noticed the same thing earlier when she went to the nurse's station to borrow a fruit knife.

Unfortunately, all of them were loaned out. But the nurse said she could get another if she tried going two floors downstairs. But Tomoe did momentarily worry she was on the wrong floor, since it had the exact same layout.

Well, that was a relatively normal situation for general hospitals with a large number of patient rooms... but Tomoe wished they'd give a little more consideration for the people who actually had to use them.
"Anyway, I'm sure a lot of hospital patients get lost.@k@vS11/28/420101219. In which case, it bothers me that these rooms don't have locks."
"Ahahaha, it's all right.@k@vS11/29/420400231. That patient earlier just made a careless mistake because he was tired."
Suddenly.
Tomoe completely froze in place, with her hand still hovering in the air reaching for the last apple piece.
And the next moment, she rose to her feet as though she had realized something, then began heading out of the hospital room without even noticing that the chair had fallen down behind her.
"W-wait, what's wrong?@k@vS11/29/420400234. Sis...?"
"...Ah, excuse me!@k@vS11/28/420101222. My name is Minai from Kakiuchi Station. I would like to speak with the First Division section chief, Honjou-san."
The timetable listing her destination pattered as she put it away.
And at last, the flight she planned to board displayed a "now commencing boarding" status.
"...Now then."
After drinking the rest of the wine in her paper cup, she slowly stood up and grabbed the bag from the seat next to her.
She had already checked her suitcase and larger luggage, so her personal belongings were relatively light.
"Hmph..."
She faintly sneered at the crowd of passengers lined up at the boarding gate.
Their seats were the poor, cramped economy seats. The services and accommodations were completely different from the first-class seat she would be sitting in.
Such a pity, sitting with your shoulders shrunk together, and having to endure it for a multiple hour long flight...
She muttered that in her head while throwing the paper cup into a nearby trash can, then turned to face the completely empty first-class gate.
Right then...
"...Hoh, vacationing in Macao, eh?@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2062. Sounds lovely~."
Looking back toward the voice that suddenly called out to her from behind, she found an elderly man in a thick coat approaching her with a pleasant smile on his face.
At a quick glance, he was styled like a worn-down business manager.
However, she understood well what his occupation was since they had met once before.
"Long time no see.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2064. It's Honjou, from the First Investigation Division of the Ibaraki prefectural police department."
The man who addressed himself as Honjou pulled a black notebook out of his jacket pocket and held it up to her.
The moment she saw it... a chill ran through Youko Hiranuma's body, as every ounce of blood froze over.
"The check-in area still looks pretty crowded.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2066. Do ya mind having a small chat until it clears up?"
"...About what?"
"Well, it's nothing major.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2068. ...There was something that's been on my mind for a long time, but I forgot about it until right this moment... Tahahah."
"...And what would that be?"

"This may be a repeat of our earlier conversation.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2070. ...When Kouhei Sawamura-kun was discovered, you first contacted the police, and then the doctor in charge... Isn't that right?"
"Yes, that's right..."
"You're not mistaken about that?"
"As I said, that's right."
There was a hint of irritation in Hiranuma's voice as she repeated that response.
What on earth was this man hoping to hear...?
"And what of it, anyway?"
"Well... isn't something odd?@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2073. How did you know Sawamura-kun had fallen to his death if all you did was look down from the eighth floor?"

"...Huh?@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2119. Isn't that perfectly natural?@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2120. The window was open and the person who was in the hospital room earlier was laying on the ground outside. Isn't it normal to think so?"

"Yes, exactly.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2075. ...But there's something that doesn't quite make sense.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2076. If that was the case, why wasn't your first thought to consider helping him?"
The smile on Honjou's face vanished as he stared at Hiranuma with a piercing glare.
...That was when she noticed other men dressed in suits like detectives were standing to block the exits.
"A fall from the eighth floor.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2078. If you think about it logically, it's high enough to cause instant death."
"...But, for professionals in the medical field, normally they'd rush to save someone even if there was only a 1% chance of success."

"In which case, shouldn't your first step have been to call emergency services,@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2081. ...and call the police only after you confirmed he was dead.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2082. And the doctor in charge would be the last person to contact.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2083. ...Or am I mistaken?"
"That's because... I was frightened and shaken up at the time..."

"And then there's the story that Sawamura-kun's room was a closed room, since the door was locked...@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2085. Indeed, the cameras showed that nobody entered his room after you, just as you said."
"So, he locked the door before accidentally falling from the room, but then you said he committed suicide."

"But the door could've been locked after it was closed.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2088. In which case, the condition for the closed room falls apart.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2089. It's possible that the perpetrator locked the door after leaving the room, using the key."

"Or to put it another way.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2091. If someone wanted to murder him and disguise it as a suicide,@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2092. the most suspicious individual is the last person to meet him. You..."
"Wh-what is this?!"
Hiranuma shouted in rage and dropped her carry-on bag to the floor. The clasp came undone and the bag split open, spilling some cosmetics and small articles to the floor.
"Then, you're suggesting I threw him out the window?@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2127. Don't be absurd. You're aware of the difference between our body types, right?"

"How can you suggest that I brought someone taller than myself to that window, much less threw him out of it?@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2129. It would be the other way around. He'd fight back, and I'd be the one thrown out!"

"Well, normally you'd think so.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2094. But... what if you think about it like this?@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2095. He approached the window on his own and climbed to the ledge.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2096. If that were the case, you'd be able to throw him off with just a light push..."
"I-I don't understand that meaning of that!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2131. Why would he put himself in such a dangerous situation?"
"That's simple.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2098. ...It's because he didn't think it was dangerous."
...Hiranuma caught her breath.
He knew... This man knew all her machinations...!

"You said that before Sawamura-kun was discovered, he had his temperature taken and then went to sleep.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2100. ...Then, there was a malfunction with the alarm just before the first to discover the body, Nagisa Ozaki, came to visit him."

"Shortly thereafter, the nurses visited every patient's room to explain the situation...@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2102. but you told Sawamura-kun something else.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2103. ...Something like, "There's a fire! Get out of here!""

"And then, you pointed toward the window.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2105. When Sawamura-kun heard that, he was confused from just waking up and thought he was still in his room on the first floor.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2106. His room was originally on the first floor, and he had only moved to the eighth floor a week before."
"And not only that, but his old room was physically located somewhere that escaping through the window would be faster than running down the hallway."
"So, as he hastily approached the window and climbed up to the ledge, he was surprised to notice he was actually on the eighth floor."
"...When he noticed he was about to fall, he grabbed the window frame with both hands. But when you approached him from behind and pushed, he didn't have the means to support himself so he fell..."
"I-is there any evidence of this...?"
"Here... do you know what these are?@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2111. They're markings from Sawamura-kun's fingernails on the window frame."

"Normally, when someone is considering jumping from a high place, the fingerprints and nail marks appear just on the bottom, rather than the left and right sides.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2113. But in his case, there were nail markings on the right side and the bottom.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2114. Moreover, he clenched so strongly on the right side that it peeled his nails off, leaving blood stains behind..."
"Y-You can't tell that for sure..."
"Also... we found traces of skin of a different blood type beneath his nails.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2116. The blood type was A-.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2117. And sure enough, that's your type too..."
"Would you mind taking off those gloves?@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2119. One of your hands should have a scar from your fight with Sawamura-kun."
"Please come with me to prefectural police headquarters."
Hiranuma hung her head, then fell to her knees.
Seeing that, Honjou silently nodded to the detectives behind her. On that signal, they moved around to both sides of her.
However.
"...@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2141.Heh, hehehehehehehehehe.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2142. Hahahahahahahahahahahahahahahaha."
Suddenly.
Hiranuma sneered and began laughing in a low, creepy voice. Then, she looked up with her eyes open wide while still cackling.
"Gyahahahahahahahahahaha!!!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2144. Gugyageragubebigyogehahahahahahahaha!!!"
"Wha? W-what is this...?!"
"Seki, Honda!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2124. Restrain the suspect!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2125. Don't let either of her hands free!!"
"Hi, hihyahahahahahahahahaha...!!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2146. Gwaahiheagahahahahahahahaha!!"
"Tch!!"

Hiranuma shook her hair about and cried out in a strange voice while picking up the fruit knife that had fallen out of her carry-on bag. Then, she swung it in all directions in an effort to intimidate the detectives.
And then, noticing the gap between the detectives she managed to open with her blade, she ran toward the back window in an effort to escape.
Then...
"Gah!!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2148. ...Hya, hi, hehihyah...!!"
She plunged the knife into her own throat... and stabbed it in over and over again.
The dull gurgling sound of an overflowing viscous liquid rang out... while a stream of bright red blood fell down to the floor.
Out of nowhere... a grief-stricken scream came out, strong enough to tear through silk.
The surrounding detectives all froze in horror. ...Even Honjou felt the urge to vomit at the miserable sight.
"H-Hondaa, call the first-aid squad!!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2129. Tell them to bring resuscitation tools and a rescue kit!!!"
"Suicide?!@k@vS11/28/420101224. ...So, what became of the suspect?"
"...I'm sorry.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2131. Emergency first aid was administered and she was transported to a hospital... but it was all for nothing."
Tomoe was so shocked she was at a loss for words after hearing that from Honjou over the phone.
Losing control and turning to suicide wasn't out of the question, naturally. Honjou and the others probably felt the same way.
But... the men froze when they witnessed Hiranuma's strange, or rather, completely baffling transformation.
Shrieking in a weird voice and going mad with laughter... before finally piercing her throat with a knife, and dying.
Nobody in decent mental health would ever turn to that means, even if they intended self-harm.
Was it a spasm induced by some illness? Or was she at her wits' end after being cornered and acting in a panic? Tomoe couldn't say.
"Have you heard if Youko Hiranuma was taking any sort of drugs?"

"No, at least not at the moment.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2133. ...Her colleagues, acquaintances, and neighbors said she had a normal working attitude. When asked about it, all they could say was, "I can't believe it."@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2134. And it didn't seem like they were covering for her."
"...But why turn to that in the end?"
"I dunno.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2136. ...Honestly, I have absolutely no idea.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2137. I don't understand Youko Hiranuma's motive for killing Kouhei Sawamura either."

"And if she was somehow involved with the Nagisa Ozaki murder case you were investigating... now everything is in the dark.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2139. Even after searching her house, we didn't find any evidence.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2140. ...We're completely cut off in all directions."
"...Honjou-san."
It was a rare occasion for Honjou to speak as though he had given up. He always attempted to search for a breakthrough that could lead to a new theory...
In other words... this was the kind of incident where the culprit could never be found.
"I'll do what I can on my end to keep searching.@k@vS11/28/420101230. Can you contact me if anything else turns up?"
"...Tomoe-chan."
"Yes?"
"This is just a hunch, but...@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2143. the truth behind this incident runs deeper than you think."
"...I understand that."
"Don't say that so casually.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2145. That's the same as saying you don't understand at all."
Honjou said that in response to Tomoe's almost reflexive answer.

"A male student was murdered and the culprit committed suicide.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2147. ...If that was all there was to it, it could simply be a case of a murder committed for personal enjoyment or to sate curiosity."
"But a person who exhibited no such abnormalities until now suddenly changed and attacked another person, then herself.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2149. ...Has there ever been an incident like this before?"
"...@k@vS11/28/420101234.No, there are no cases like this, even among Western case records."
Criminals who kill to sate curiosity always have some sort of preference or an established environment that led them to that thought.
There's no chance of a person turning to aberrant behavior without that.

"...Be careful, Tomoe-chan.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2151. This isn't the kind of thing you should handle on your own in your spare time.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2152. Always be cognizant of your surroundings."
"Right.@k@vS11/28/420101236. ...I'll keep that in the back of my mind, always."
Then, Tomoe hung up the phone.
"Shouldn't handle on my own...huh?"
That was certainly true.
But two children that she knew had already fallen victim.
Could she dismiss the case as being blocked on all sides, just as he said...?
There was another incident where a person not showing any abnormalities suddenly changed and attacked a person...
Even though she had convinced herself there was "no such thing" just a moment ago. ...But isn't the Reina Ryuugu case a perfect example of that?!
"If that's the case, then that girl...!"
...There was a possibility...!
That Reina Ryuugu had to have some connection to this series of incidents...!!
In which case, if she investigated the girl's background a little more thoroughly... surely...!
Just then, the phone directly in front of her began to ring. Though she was confused for a moment by the sudden interruption, Tomoe quickly switched back to normal and picked up the receiver.
"Yes, hello."
"Assistant Director Minai, Chief Yamaoki is calling for you.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_soumuA2002. Please come to the chief's office."
"...The chief?"
Having received a call from the general affairs office, likely needing her to act as Madoka's representative in her absence, Tomoe got up and rose to her feet.
